element_introduction_order.element: Object pickup and throw mechanics (acorns, crates, barrels, balls)

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Unlocking new paths through broken barriers

typical_relief_triggers: Support character appearances (Monterey Jack breaking barriers)
Gadget's helpful advice and contact points
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Speed-focused gameplay with moderate challenge; reviewers noted fast-paced action with variable difficulty perception (criticized as too easy in NA, praised as tough in EU)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory, teaching core mechanics of movement and object throwing
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate challenge with support mechanics introduced (Monterey Jack, Zipper); variable perception between regions
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters and final Fat Cat confrontation requiring mastery of all mechanics
notable_spikes: Fat Cat final hideout sequence requiring rocket travel

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Linear progression with support character gates
notable_level_patterns.description: Levels progressed linearly through streets and laboratory, with Monterey Jack appearing to break barricades that block advancement, creating natural progression gates
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Streets and laboratory sections early game

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels begin with safe exploration and object pickup, escalate to moderate enemy density, culminate in boss encounters
Players choose which levels to access via a map of various locations throughout the city, allowing non-linear level order selection
Quest progression through streets → laboratory → casino → final hideout; each zone thematically matches narrative progression
